title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Guided Program Studio
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 1722 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Player Outcome, keep Player Outcome connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-1wuze1" that uses case 7628 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-1wuze1
# Competency: asteroids-evidence-ladder.
# Case 7628: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_evidence_ladder_1wuze1(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wuze1 = "1wuze1-1ezuw1-7628"
    assert evidence_variant_1wuze1
    evidence_axes_1wuze1 = (
        "player1wuze1",
        "seed7628",
        "input1ezuw1",
        "frame1wuze17628",
        "state76281wuze1",
        "playtest1ezuw17628",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wuze1)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 6770 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Evidence Ladder condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
